title: Bull Run in Loans Slams CLO Equity Buyers, Says Eagle Point’s Majewski (Podcast)
summary: Collateralized loan obligations face a second straight year of equity losses, according to Eagle Point Credit Management, a major buyer. “Where we stand right now, it’s certainly possible,” Tom Majewski, the $14 billion private-credit manager’s founder and managing partner, says in this episode of the Credit Edge podcast, when asked about the chance of negative returns. “While there were some credit problems, oddly the bigger issue was the bull market and repricing of loans,” Majewski tells Bloo

title: Asian Stocks to Track US Slide After Chip Selloff: Markets Wrap
summary: Stocks in Asia looked set to open lower Friday after a selloff in chipmakers dragged down Wall Street as investors questioned whether massive artificial-intelligence investments can justify lofty valuations.

title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 17
summary: Oil extended a weekly advance as the escalating conflict between the US and Iran raised concerns about disruptions to supply from the Middle East.

title: Intuitive Surgical Drops as ACA Changes, GLP-1s Weigh on Growth
summary: Intuitive Surgical shares fell as much as 13% in after-market trading as growth in use of its da Vinci surgical robots slowed to the weakest pace in four years and its guidance for procedure growth was unchanged.

title: Bank Rally Hits Short Sellers Betting on Australia Lending Slump
summary: The upward march in share prices of Australia’s biggest banks is reversing the tumble sparked two months ago by property tax changes, and inflicting pain on short sellers who were anticipating a prolonged plunge.

title: CoreLogic in Talks to Sweeten Terms on Struggling Debt Sale
summary: CoreLogic Inc. is considering offering sweeter terms on at least a portion of its $5.3 billion debt deal after receiving lukewarm investor demand.

title: How the Iran War Is Rewiring the Oil Market
summary: The US-Israel war with Iran was supposed to blow up energy markets, but that hasn’t happened yet. In this weekly documentary, Bloomberg Originals explains why.

title: Alcoa Shares Tumble After Cyclone Cuts Into Alumina Output
summary: Alcoa Corp. cut its production forecast for alumina after operational problems at an Australian refinery weighed on output, overshadowing a quarter in which higher aluminum prices helped lift the metal producer’s revenue.

title: Emerging Currencies Fall as US, Iran Tensions Strengthen Dollar
summary: Most developing-world currencies fell Thursday as the US launched fresh strikes against Iran, sparking a flight to haven assets that pushed the dollar higher.

title: Sweetgreen Shares Slide to Three-Month Low on Cyclosporiasis Outbreak Fears
summary: Sweetgreen Inc. has lost more than a quarter of its market value in just four days as investors dump the stock due to fears about outbreak of cyclosporiasis — an intestinal infection caused by parasites in raw produce and water that has sickened thousands across the US.

title: Chip Stocks Get Hit as AI Spending Anxiety Builds: Markets Wrap
summary: A selloff in chipmakers dragged down stocks amid concerns over whether massive artificial-intelligence investments will justify lofty valuations, with a resurgence in geopolitical risks also weighing on sentiment.

title: Gold Falls as US-Iran Hostilities Rekindles Fed Hike Bets
summary: Gold declined as escalating tensions in the Middle East rekindled expectations that the Federal Reserve may need to hike interest rates to contain stubborn inflation.

title: Bonds Have a Big Tech Problem of Their Own
summary: Concentration risk is becoming a thing.

title: Bond Traders Bail on Fed Hike Bets on Softer Inflation Path
summary: The bullish tone in the US bond market following two benign inflation reports has traders of interest-rate options scrambling to exit positions bought in anticipation of at least one rate hike by the Federal Reserve this year.

title: Why IPOs of Tiny Foreign Companies Have Vanished in US
summary: Added scrutiny on foreign microcaps puts a damper on public debuts

title: SpaceX Post-Listing Collapse Threatens IPO Market’s AI Euphoria
summary: SpaceX’s fall from its post-listing peak to below its IPO price in just a month poured cold water on the market for newly public companies, dragging a key gauge of this year’s debuts down with it.

title: A Sports-Betting ETF Is Like Old-School Stockpicking
summary: Proposed prediction market ETFs risk missing the point

title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 16
summary: Oil dipped, though remains higher for the week, as traders balanced profit-taking with concerns about disruptions to Middle East flows.

title: Oil Traders Crowd Into Cheap TACO Hedge as Iran War Amps Up
summary: An unusual options trade is becoming a favored hedge against the risks of sudden U-turns in US President Donald Trump’s stance on Iran, a dynamic that has repeatedly whipsawed markets in recent months.

title: China Beef Curbs Start Rippling Across Brazil Meat Industry
summary: Brazil is starting to feel the impact of China’s beef import quota, with lower shipments to the country’s largest overseas market reverberating across the entire supply chain.

title: Korea ETF’s (EWY) Record Inflow Fuels SK Hynix Proxy Play
summary: A record inflow hit the largest US-listed South Korea exchange-traded fund, as investors seeking exposure to SK Hynix Inc. piled into the ETF as a proxy at a time when the chipmaker’s new American depositary receipts trade at a substantial premium to its local shares.
